person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
CAUTION! NO PARENTS, SPECIFIC ORIGINS, WIFE OR CHILDREN IDENTIFIED BY THE GREAT MIGRATION STUDY PROJECT!

Great Migration 1634-1635, A-B. (Online database. AmericanAncestors.org. New England Historic Genealogical Society, 2008.) Originally published as: The Great Migration, Immigrants to New England, 1634-1635, Volume 1, A-B, by Robert Charles Anderson, George F. Sanborn, Jr., and Melinde Lutz Sanborn. Boston: New England Historic Genealogical Society, 1999.

https://www.americanancestors.org/DB114/i/7051/253/22074598

https://www.americanancestors.org/publications/great-migration-study-project/critical-acclaim

=== Unverified Content ===

I've seen perhaps a half dozen fathers purposed for Edward virtually all from various places in England. But never have I seen supporting documentation. If someone wants to solve this puzzle, I would recommend that an eligible male-line Bennett descendant do a Y-DNA test. The result will match him to the right family at the Bennett DNA Project at FTDNA.com. Obviously, the paper trail is not going to do that.

======================================

We think this person is Edward Bennett II, son of Edward Bennett I born around 1580

[ Please provide reasoning for that statement. ]

====================================

DO NOT MERGE THIS PERSON WITH OTHER EDWARD'S OF THE SAME NAME-SEE RESEARCH ATTACHED-NO KNOWN PARENTS!!!!!
Immigration of Edward and Elizabeth___ Bennett to Colonial America  From papers belonging to Rev. George S Bennett, was furnished the following: Edward Bennett, born in England, came with his wife Elizabeth, and four children, and settled in Weymouth, Mass. in 1636, where land was given to him. He was made a freeman at the General Court held in Boston, 26 May 1636. He resided in Weymouth, Massachusetes Bay Colony for seven years, when he joined the Reverend Newman's company, and became one of the original proprietors of that part of Rehoboth, Massachusettes, called Seakonk, near Providence, R.I., moving there with his family in 1643. He died in 1645-6. His children who were born in England were: Samuel, born 1628, England; John, 1630; England; Priscilla, 1632 in England who married William Carpenter, 5 October 1651; name of fourth child not given. 
      The older two Bennett sons left Rehoboth by 1658: "By then, son Samuel had moved to Rhode Island and son John, to Connecticut. Daughter Priscilla married William Carpenter on October 5, 1651, and died on October 20, 1663, the day their son Benjamin was born. Edward and Richard Bennett, the younger sons, stayed in Rehoboth but later the Bennett name fades from town records."

New Information: Correct Christening Edward Bennett,24 August 1595 Highworth,Wiltshire, England, Father: Henry Bennett: Pedigree Resource File. Stephen King

zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Bennett於大約1594年出生在英國多瑟特維姆斯時，他的父親William Thomas Bennett39歲，母親Joan Hodgson36歲。他於1622年10月27日與Elizabeth Alice Eginton在英國斯塔福爾瑟格利結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1636年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯。他去世於1645年6月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯享年52歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Bennett于大约1594年出生在英国多瑟特維姆斯时，他的父亲William Thomas Bennett39岁，母亲Joan Hodgson36岁。他于1622年10月27日与Elizabeth Alice Eginton在英国斯塔福爾瑟格利结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他于1636年住在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼維姆斯。他去世于1645年6月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯享年52岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼布里斯托雷沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Bennett
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the medieval personal name Benedict , from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. In the 12th century the Latin form of the name is found in England alongside versions derived from the Old French form Beneit, Benoit, which was common among the Normans. The surname has also been established in Ireland since the 14th century.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Bernhard  .
Altered form of French Benet or Bennet or of some other similar (like-sounding) French surname. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

variants.name: Bennet
meanings: English: variant of   Bennett  .
French: derivative of   Benne  .

variants.name: Bennitt
meanings: English (Midlands): variant of   Bennett  .

variants.name: Bennetts
meanings: English (Cornwall): variant of Bennett , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Dittus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Fritz, Kurt, Otto, Reinhold.
German: from a vernacular short form of the Latin personal name Benedictus (see Bennett ).

variants.name: Benedict
meanings: English, German, and Dutch: from the personal name Benedict, from Latin Benedictus ‘blessed’. This owed its popularity in the Middle Ages chiefly to Saint Benedict of Norcia (c. 480–550), who founded the Benedictine order of monks at Monte Cassino and wrote a monastic rule that formed a model for all subsequent rules. No doubt the meaning of the Latin word also contributed to its popularity as a personal name, especially in Romance countries. Occasionally the English surname may derive from Latin benedicite ‘bless (you)’, perhaps given as a nickname to an habitual user of the expression. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed the German variant Benedikt and many cognates from other languages, e.g. Hungarian Benedek , Slovenian Benedik (see Benedick ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Italian Benedetti .

variants.name: Bennette
meanings: English (Cornwall): altered form of   Bennett  .

variants.name: Benett
meanings: English: variant of   Bennett  .

variants.name: Bennight
meanings: Probably an altered form of English   Bennett  .

variants.name: Bennison
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Bene(i)t + -son. See Bennett and compare Benson .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Bennett was born about 1594, in Weymouth, Dorset, England, his father, William Thomas Bennett, was 39 and his mother, Joan Hodgson, was 36. He married Elizabeth Alice Eginton on 27 October 1622, in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He lived in Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1636. He died in June 1645, in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 52, and was buried in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Bennett est né environ 1594, à Weymouth, Dorset, Angleterre, son père, William Thomas Bennett, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Joan Hodgson, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Alice Eginton le 27 octobre 1622, à Sedgley, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il a vécu à Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1636. Il est décédé en juin 1645, à Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterré à Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Bennett um 1594 in Weymouth, Dorset, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Thomas Bennett 39 und seine Mutter Joan Hodgson 36. Er hat am 27. Oktober 1622 in Sedgley, Staffordshire, England Elizabeth Alice Eginton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1636 hat er in Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America gelebt. Er starb im Juni 1645 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Bennett è nato il circa 1594 nel Weymouth, Dorset, England, il suo padre, William Thomas Bennett, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Joan Hodgson, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Alice Eginton il 27 ottobre 1622, nel Sedgley, Staffordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Vissuto a Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1636. Morì nel giugno 1645, nel Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolto a Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Bennettが約1594年にイギリスドールセトウエイモウスで生まれた時，父William Thomas Bennettは39歳，母Joan Hodgsonは36歳であった。1622年10月27日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアセドグリーでElizabeth Alice Egintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1636年にはイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーウエイモウスに住んでいた。彼は1645年6月にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーブリストルレhオボスで52歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーブリストルレhオボスにて埋葬された。

ko.Rehoboth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스
ko.Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Brit: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 베이모우스
ko.Rehoboth, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Bennett(이)가 대략 1594년 영국 도르세트 베이모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Thomas Bennett(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Hodgson(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1622년 10월 27일 영국 스태퍼드실르 세지리에서 Elizabeth Alice Eginton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1636년도에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 베이모우스에서 살았다. 그는 1645년 6월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 브리스톨 레홉스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Bennett nasceu aproximadamente 1594, em Weymouth, Dorset, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Thomas Bennett, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Joan Hodgson, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Alice Eginton em 27 de outubro de 1622, em Sedgley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele viveu em Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1636. Ele faleceu em junho de 1645, em Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, com 52 anos, e foi sepultado em Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Bennett родился около 1594 г. в местности вимоут, дорсет, Англия, его отцу по имени William Thomas Bennett было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Hodgson, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Alice Eginton 27 октября 1622 г. в местности седжли, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он жил в местности вимоут, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1636 году. Он умер июнь 1645 г. в местности рэобот, бристол, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 52 лет и был похоронен в местности рэобот, бристол, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Bennett nació aproximadamente 1594, en Weymouth, Dorset, Inglaterra, su papá, William Thomas Bennett, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Joan Hodgson, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Alice Eginton el 27 de octubre de 1622, en Sedgley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Vivió en Weymouth, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1636. Murió en junio de 1645, en Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultado en Rehoboth, Bristol, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.
